Hey, its our paper!

Most people, including really accomplished people, don't have an accurate mental model of how LLMs operate (and why would they?)

You see this in wide beliefs that AI is just copying from known sources, or that it only produces average answers, or that it can't generate new ideas

Great move. Every “automated” social media strategy has a useful like of 3 months tops. Every platform will treat AI content as spam and work to remove it. 

The only sustainable content strategy in the AI future is to be real, authentic, and to build trust.

AI will be able to build anything for anyone.

To do this AI will use building blocks.

Building blocks, that AI is familiar with, that come with extensive instructions. 

A lot of what you need a standalone platform for today, will be converted into building blocks with

The messy stages are the most exciting.

If you wait for the neat and tidy stage, you’ll be competing with everyone.

Emotionless AI is getting left behind.

The next wave won’t just answer prompts, it’ll read tone, adapt emotionally, and make every interaction feel alive.

Zeely is turning AI from “tool” into “experience.”

Drops in 2 days. Don’t blink.

Announcing @a16z's investment in Town 🎉

It's an AI assistant that truly knows you.

Since I joined Town a few months ago, it's run my work life - emails, to-do lists, meeting prep, reminders.

It can actually DO things for you and has full context.
 https://t.co/oFgwdIegGF

@a16z Town connects to the places you do work (email, Slack, WhatsApp, and much more). 

It handles your recurring tasks, but also proposes things that it can tackle for you.

More from me + @arampell on why we're so excited to invest in Town 👇

https://t.co/BfjA3GVFCP

New open weight image model just dropped ✨

I've been playing with Ideogram 4.0 over the past few days - and it's insanely impressive. 

The model can render text really well, generates high res images, and is exceptional at design.

A few examples 👇 https://t.co/HIzVnX3ZqY

One of the great things about this model is that it can take both short and long prompts - and adhere to them very well. 

In the WIRED image above, I gave the model the entire copy. 

In the other two, I gave one vague sentence of guidance and the model made the rest. https://t.co/B5knE8oaOl

这几天在看房子，感受到北京一个微妙的变化
买房的人少了，租房的人多了
房东说他要自己住，不跟我们续租了
然后看了三天新房子，终于相中一套
新房东说他要留给儿子住，突然不想租了
三年前，租房随便挑的，一天能看十套
今年，看完一圈竟然没有找到合适的

skill 的下一代形态可能是什么？

技能包📦应该如何封装，才能既方便传播，又可商业化？

plugin？

浏览器的扩展机制是不是可能的答案？

以 llm 基础，看到有两条发展路径：

一条是往下走，原子化，把一个人的能力给拆成一个个针对具体任务的技能包，供用户调用。

一个是往上走，组件化，把一个场景的最佳实践（workflow 、节点优化、技能包）给封装起来，供需求方调用。
